Description: A form of inflammatory arthritis characterized by sudden, severe attacks of pain, redness, and tenderness in joints caused by deposition of urate crystals.
Symptoms: Intense joint pain, often in the big toe
Causes: High levels of uric acid in the blood
Diet rich in purines (red meat, seafood)

Description: A chronic condition characterized by high blood sugar levels due to the body's inability to produce or use insulin effectively.
A condition in which the force of the blood against the artery walls is too high, often called high blood pressure.
A chronic respiratory disease marked by inflammation and narrowing of the airways, leading to wheezing, shortness of breath, and coughing.
Symptoms: Coughing, especially at night or early morning

Description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract, typically causing nasal congestion, sneezing, and sore throat.
A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses that infect the nose, throat, and sometimes the lungs.
Causes: Influenza A, B, or C viruses

Description: An infection that inflames air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
Inflammation of the lining of bronchial tubes, which carry air to and from the lungs.
A serious bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs but can spread to other parts of the body.
A group of lung diseases that block airflow and make breathing difficult, including emphysema and chronic bronchitis.
Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, typically resulting from bacterial toxins or viral infection.
Sores that develop on the lining of the stomach, upper small intestine, or esophagus.
A common disorder affecting the large intestine, causing cramping, abdominal pain, bloating, gas, diarrhea, and constipation.
Inflammation of the liver, most commonly caused by viral infection but can also result from alcohol, medications, or autoimmune conditions.
Causes: Hepatitis A, B, C, D, or E viruses

Description: Late stage of scarring (fibrosis) of the liver caused by many forms of liver diseases and conditions.
Hard deposits of minerals and salts that form inside the kidneys and can cause severe pain when passing through the urinary tract.
Symptoms: Severe pain in side and back
Pain radiating to lower abdomen and groin
Causes: High levels of calcium, oxalate, or uric acid

Description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, most commonly affecting the bladder and urethra.
Symptoms: Passing frequent, small amounts of urine

Description: Gradual loss of kidney function over time, which can lead to kidney failure if not treated.
Symptoms: Swelling in legs, ankles, or feet

Description: An autoimmune disorder that causes chronic inflammation of the joints and surrounding tissues.
The most common form of arthritis, caused by wear and tear of joint cartilage over time.
A condition that weakens bones, making them fragile and more likely to break.
A neurological condition characterized by recurrent, severe headaches often accompanied by nausea, vomiting, and sensitivity to light and sound.
A neurological disorder marked by recurrent, unprovoked seizures.
A progressive nervous system disorder that affects movement, causing tremors, stiffness, and difficulty with balance and coordination.
A progressive neurodegenerative disorder that causes brain cells to degenerate and die, leading to memory loss and cognitive decline.
Causes: Plaques and tangles in the brain

Description: A mood disorder that causes persistent feelings of sadness, loss of interest, and affects how you feel, think, and handle daily activities.
A group of mental health conditions characterized by excessive worry, fear, or anxiety that interferes with daily activities.
A mental health condition that causes extreme mood swings including emotional highs (mania) and lows (depression).
A serious mental disorder that affects how a person thinks, feels, and behaves, often involving psychosis.
A condition where plaque builds up inside the coronary arteries, reducing blood flow to the heart muscle.
A condition where the heart cannot pump enough blood to meet the body's needs.
Symptoms: Swelling in legs, ankles, and feet

Description: Abnormal heart rhythms that can cause the heart to beat too fast, too slow, or irregularly.
A medical emergency that occurs when blood flow to the brain is interrupted, causing brain cell death.
A condition in which the blood lacks adequate healthy red blood cells to carry sufficient oxygen to body tissues.
A type of cancer that affects the blood and bone marrow, causing abnormal production of white blood cells.
Conditions affecting the thyroid gland, including hyperthyroidism (overactive) and hypothyroidism (underactive).
A condition where the thyroid gland doesn't produce enough thyroid hormone, slowing down metabolism.
A condition where the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, speeding up metabolism.
An infection of the bone, usually caused by bacteria, that can lead to bone destruction.
Symptoms: Swelling and warmth over affected area

Description: A common bacterial skin infection that causes redness, swelling, and pain in the affected area.
A chronic skin condition that causes inflamed, itchy, and red patches on the skin.
A chronic autoimmune skin condition that causes rapid skin cell growth, leading to thick, scaly patches.
A common skin condition that occurs when hair follicles become plugged with oil and dead skin cells.
Causes: Hair follicles clogged by oil and dead skin

Description: Inflammation of the skin that can have various causes, including contact with irritants or allergens.
Inflammation or infection of the transparent membrane (conjunctiva) that lines the eyelid and covers the white part of the eyeball.
Symptoms: Redness in one or both eyes

Description: Clouding of the normally clear lens of the eye, leading to blurred vision.
A group of eye conditions that damage the optic nerve, often due to abnormally high pressure in the eye.
An eye disease that causes vision loss in the center of the field of vision, typically affecting older adults.
Symptoms: Dark or empty areas in central vision

Description: Infection or inflammation of the middle ear, commonly affecting children.
Inflammation or swelling of the tissue lining the sinuses, which can be acute or chronic.
Inflammation of the tonsils, usually caused by viral or bacterial infection.
Inflammation of the voice box (larynx) that causes hoarseness or loss of voice.
Inflammation of the appendix, a small pouch attached to the large intestine, requiring emergency treatment.
Symptoms: Sudden pain in lower right abdomen

Description: Hardened deposits in the gallbladder that can cause pain and complications.
Symptoms: Sudden pain in upper right abdomen

Description: Inflammation of the pancreas, which can be acute or chronic.
Inflammation or infection of small pouches (diverticula) that can form in the walls of the colon.
Swollen veins in the lower rectum and anus that can cause discomfort and bleeding.
A highly contagious viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus, characterized by an itchy, blister-like rash.
A viral infection caused by the varicella-zoster virus (same virus that causes chickenpox), causing a painful rash.
A highly contagious viral infection that causes a characteristic rash and can lead to serious complications.
A viral infection that primarily affects the salivary glands, causing painful swelling.
A contagious viral infection that causes a distinctive red rash, also known as German measles.
An infectious disease commonly called 'mono' or 'kissing disease,' caused by the Epstein-Barr virus.
A bacterial infection transmitted through the bite of infected black-legged ticks.
A life-threatening disease caused by parasites transmitted to people through the bites of infected mosquitoes.
A mosquito-borne viral infection that causes flu-like illness and can develop into severe dengue.
A viral disease transmitted to humans by infected mosquitoes, causing fever and severe joint pain.
A mosquito-borne viral infection that can cause birth defects if contracted during pregnancy.
A respiratory illness caused by the novel coronavirus SARS-CoV-2, first identified in 2019.
A serious bacterial infection that causes painful muscle stiffness and can be life-threatening.
A serious bacterial infection that affects the mucous membranes of the throat and nose.
A highly contagious respiratory tract infection characterized by severe coughing fits.
Inflammation of the membranes (meninges) surrounding the brain and spinal cord, which can be life-threatening.
Inflammation of the brain, usually caused by a viral infection, which can be life-threatening.
Causes: Viral infections (herpes simplex, West Nile, etc.)

Description: A life-threatening condition that occurs when the body's response to an infection damages its own tissues and organs.
Inflammation of the inner lining of the heart chambers and valves, usually caused by bacterial infection.
A condition that weakens bones, making them fragile and more likely to break.
A disorder characterized by widespread musculoskeletal pain accompanied by fatigue, sleep, memory, and mood issues.
A chronic autoimmune disease that can damage any part of the body, including skin, joints, and organs.
